# quickthink

Local-first inference control layer that adds a compressed planning scaffold
to small LLMs, improving structured output reliability with latency-aware routing.

## What it is

quickthink wraps Ollama-backed LLM calls with a lightweight scaffold:
- `lite` mode (default): one-pass inline plan prefix + answer in one generation
- `two_pass` mode: separate plan call then answer call
- `direct` mode: no planning pass, raw prompt to model

Preset routing profiles (`fast`, `balanced`, `strict`) and lane policies
(`default`, `strict_safe`) let you tune the overhead-vs-quality tradeoff.

## When to use

- You run local LLMs via Ollama and want more reliable structured outputs
- You need latency-aware routing that can bypass planning for short prompts
- You want a canonical eval harness (run/judge/validate/report) for reproducible
  project-level quality checks

## When not to use

- You need hosted API calls (this is local-first only)
- You want a full agent orchestration platform
- You need guaranteed performance improvements — gains are model/task dependent

## Install

```
pip install quickthink
```

Requires Ollama running locally: https://ollama.com/

## Key commands

```bash
# Ask with compressed planning
quickthink ask "Design a retry strategy" --model qwen2.5:1.5b

# Show plan prefix in terminal
quickthink ask "Design a retry strategy" --show-plan

# Two-pass mode with routing diagnostics
quickthink ask "Design a retry strategy" --mode two_pass --show-route

# Benchmark three modes
quickthink bench "Is 2+2=4?" --runs 3

# Launch local eval UI
quickthink ui
# then open http://127.0.0.1:7860

# List supported models
quickthink list-models

# List preset profiles
quickthink list-presets
```

## Supported models

- qwen2.5:1.5b
- mistral:7b
- gemma3:27b

## Source

https://github.com/hermes-labs-ai/quickthink
Apache-2.0. Part of the Hermes Labs audit infrastructure stack.
